text:
Professor: Alexander Russell
Overall rating: 4.2/5 | Would take again (%): 63% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.5 | Top tags: Lots of Homework, Test Heavy, Tough Grader

Professor: Alexander Russell
This class is super hard. Entire grade is based on 4 exams which require a ton of studying. Professor Russell isn't a bad professor and his lectures are okay but be prepared for long assignments and really difficult exams. The format was usually 5 questions and you chose 4 to be graded. Studying was unclear and it was hard to prepare in general.

I think he's probably one of the greatest teachers I have ever had. I tend to skip classes, but I went to every single one of his classes because he's a joy to listen to. His assignments are hard. Exams are not very difficult and are designed with time constraints in mind. Same deal with 5852 as they are the same classes.

Professor: Alexander Russell
I wouldn't take this if you took cse3400 and don't plan on going to grad school. You learn the same topics and it's more difficult. Spent up to 10 hours doing HWs each week. 50% of our grade was directed by 2 quizzes (which quizzed you on new material). For my concentration (cryptography), I feel like I wasted my semester not learning any new stuff

Hardest class I've taken at UConn so far. Would not suggest taking unless he changed the grading since I've taken it

The class is very difficult but you should be alright if you go to lecture and do all the problem sets on time. Go to lecture; its worth your time. Professor Russell is very intelligent and an excellent lecturer. I highly recommend him for CSE 1729.

A very difficult class but if you put in the effort in homework and lab, you'll be fine. Professor clearly cares about his students, and although I never attended office hours, he answered questions on the online discussion board promptly and thoroughly. Good lectures.

Professor: Alexander Russell
Really good lectures, he's very engaging and is great at presenting the material. He understands and answers all the questions asked too. Homeworks and exams were tough but not impossible, just requires the extra effort and he grades fairly.

Professor Russell is SMART. He has quite the impressive list of research papers and he cares about his students. I would suggest taking any and all of his classes as possible. That being said, it's not going to be easy, but you'll learn a lot.

This is a challenging class about dry material, but Prof. Russell did a superb job at teaching. Prof. Russell is easily one of the top 3 CSE professors at Uconn. His precision and speech skills are unrivaled. The few number of reviews he has is a testament that few are left disappointed after his classes, since most reviews are revengeful on here.

Professor: Alexander Russell
Alex Russel is the funniest, most lively and most passionate professor I've ever had. He takes dry content and makes it interesting. However, the material is obscenely hard to understand (not because of his teaching, its just very abstract). Fantastic professor, you will learn a lot, but even good students will have a hard time getting an A.

Professor: David Strimple
Overall rating: 1.2/5 | Would take again (%): 8% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.9 | Top tags: Tough Grader, Graded by few things, Test Heavy

Professor: David Strimple
If you are new to computer science, Python, or coding in general, be prepared to teach yourself a majority of the topics in this course.

For teaching such an introductory-level course, he expects you to know so much. The funny thing is, I didn't learn a single thing from this guy at all! The online software this course uses (ZyBooks) is the only reason why I passed. Not only does he not teach, but when you ask him questions, he is just so rude and dismissive. Take anyone but him.

Taking this class made me go to SHAW to try and better my mental health. Prof. Strimple without a doubt is the worst professor I've ever had in my lifetime. He never teaches the class in lectures, he just reads really bad slides and then talks about his life. He will also set up office hours; when you go nobody is there to assist you. Just awful.

Professor: David Strimple
He is one of the worst teachers I've ever had. He seems to be learning the content as he teaches us, and can't help us when we are confused. He's difficult to contact, and his poor teaching led many of kids including myself to fail multiple assignments and exams. He single-handedly ruined my major for me, so much so, I switched. Anybody but him.

He's absolutely awful. His office hours are a waste of time and his lectures are a waste of breath. If you want to understand foundations of code in python by any means, find someone else to teach it. He's in there at the same level of comprehension as someone who simply knows how to spell the word python. 0/10 would absolutely never recommend.

Professor: David Strimple
While I don't think he was quite as bad as some of these reviews make it seem, there is definitely room for improvement. He is definitely a harsh grader and will take points off for very minor mistakes. Regardless, I will say I do feel like I learned a lot from him and his class. Nice person too, don't be afraid to talk to him during office hours.

Horrible professor. Homework is difficult and hard to understand because of how he writes it. Talks too much without even teaching properly. Does examples in class and somehow will mix himself up and do it wrong. Grades horribly because he wants it to be exactly how he does it and any other way is wrong.

Professor: David Strimple
1 is not low enough for this class made me want to die. This professor is nowhere near a professional and honestly should've chosen a different career. He doesn't know what he is teaching and you will struggle to comprehend the way this professor grades his assignment. I am warning everyone for your own sake do not take this course, thank me later

The capacities of this platform are insufficient to express the pure vitriol I hold for this caricature of a class. I'll withhold, simply because I do not think Strimple is inherently a bad man. That being said, he makes a Professor like Jerry Shi look like a shining example of what the CS Department should aspire to be. Ignominiously disgraceful.

Professor: David Strimple
I was very excited for 3200. BUT the lectures are difficult to follow because he makes errors constantly and most relied on videos he posted last year. Expect an announcement each class fixing errors he made. HW instructions are vague. Historically he cares more about catching students cheating than producing quality study material, do better pls

Professor: Derek Aguiar
Overall rating: 3.5/5 | Would take again (%): 59% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.1 | Top tags: Amazing lectures, Clear grading criteria, Hilarious

Professor: Derek Aguiar
The homework in the latter half of the course was a bit difficult for me, but that is only because I've never taken CS before (and thus, it takes a bit of work to learn, just like any other class). Everything in the first half of the semester was really easy. TONS of extra credit opportunities during lab times -- take advantage of it!

Makes CSE 1010 way too hard for an intro course. While you are supposed to be learning python basics, by the end of the course you will be covering other programming languages and learning about data structures that are not required for this course, yet Derek makes you cover it anyways. Choose another professor if you want an actual intro course.

I mean hes a good guy but he made the class too hard for an intro level class. I am a bio major so why do I have to know about data structures, way too hard.

Professor: Derek Aguiar
Very difficult for an intro level class, especially since the majority of students who have to take it may not have prior coding experience. You have to pretty much rely on the textbook for everything, especially since it's online. Online discussion board was more confusing than beneficial. Lab TAs weren't too helpful either and can be snarky.

Derek seems like he's passionate about what he does, but cse1010 is all based on the interactive textbook. the lectures are helpful if you're stuck, but you can get by without them. as someone else said, if you can figure out list comprehension, the midterm and final are fine. get ready for some long hw assignments though...

If you have the opportunity to, definitely take it with Derek! He's an awesome guy and you learn so much. Would recommend!

Awesome algorithms professor, you will genuinely learn so much if you put effort into this class. Definitely recommend.

Professor: Derek Aguiar
People say Aguiar's section is harder, but his grading is very forgiving. Homework is mostly completion if you follow the guidelines. On exams are as long as you follow the general formula, you'll get most points even if your solution is wrong. The final lets you recover earlier exam points. Attend lecture and do the homework and you'll be fine.

Derek is very approachable and easy to talk to! His lectures can sometimes move too quickly or be unclear, but he records them and makes them available. He's also a lenient grader and fair, I got some problems completely wrong on the first exam and still got over half credit for them. If you're proactive and put in effort you'll do well

Professor: Derek Aguiar
If you put in a TON of work, you will learn a lot from this course, and it will be useful. However, it doesn't make sense how taking this class with Aguiar compared to other professors is so much more difficult. He is passionate and wants everyone to learn, yes. But this could almost be a separate course entirely compared to the other sections.

Professor: Justin Furuness
Overall rating: 4.5/5 | Would take again (%): 89% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.6 | Top tags: Caring, Helpful, Tough Grader

Professor: Justin Furuness
Justin is a new professor but good at explaining and very knowledgable about what he does. My only complaint is that occasionally announcement were made exclusively on Discord and not emailed, which lead to me along with half my lecture coming in confused on the first cancellation.

Absolutely goated professor! Not only does he know the material, but he is willing to teach you more about Python and give opportunities to become an even better programmer. If you are struggling in this course, the best tips I would say is to come in during office hours, do the practice tests, and look for resources and videos online. Good luck!

Justin more than teaches, he helps you understand and apply the true purpose of the content. He knows everything inside and out, and is incredibly helpful at breaking down any problem/difficulty, explaining it in a simple to comprehend and apply way. He is an awesome person too, with enjoyable lectures. If given the option, I'd take him every time.

Professor: Justin Furuness
Of all the computer science professors I have taken at UConn, he stands above the rest. It felt like he was the first professor who cared about your knowledge of computer science and success post grad. For those stating that exams weigh too much, suck it up. It's the department's solution to cheating instead of letting AI prompt engineers graduate.

He was lenient with grading, available after class, and took student feedback seriously. Put at least a little bit of effort into the class, don't overuse AI, ask him questions, and actually remember material from 3100 to be successful in his class. Highly recommend him as a professor

Exams shouldn't outweigh projects by such a large margin in this course. CS should enhance students' ability to reasonably utilize AI to help them complete large-scale projects with thousands of lines of code, rather than having them lose all points for a question in exam just because they forgot a symbol and the code couldn't be compiled.

Professor: Justin Furuness
Nice person.The exams make up a large portion of the overall grade. The syllabus also states that scoring below 60 on any single exam results in failing the course. I found the grading policy difficult to understand, especially with changes during the semester. If I had other options, I might have chosen a different course.

I was unable to get into the class this fall but ended up continuing to go to the lectures because it was just a really good class. He knows the content backwards and forwards and has good feel for how students perceive the material. He saw me outside class and gave me material/help in the course even though I was not enrolled. Great instructor.

He is knowledgeable and kind, but CSE 3500 with him is stressful. Its all programming, no proofs, to prepare you for interviews in the industry. He grades strictly and exams are worth 80 percent of the grade. If you want a challenge or improve programming skills I would recommend, otherwise go with someone else!

Professor: Justin Furuness
A great professor who I've learned loads from! UConn does a pretty poor ob of making sure their CS students are competent programmers, and Justin is fixing that in my opinion. I enjoyed the class, and it was one of the few I've taken where I felt like the knowledge was worth what I was paying for.

Professor: Laurent Michel
Overall rating: 4.4/5 | Would take again (%): 86% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.1 | Top tags: Amazing lectures, Hilarious, Accessible outside class

Professor: Laurent Michel
1729 is a tough class, but Professor Michel managed to simplify the concepts, and was very engaging. He expects a lot out of students, based on difficulty of HW and the exams, but the curves are more than fair. Exams are built to test your actual coding ability, so rigorously studying the textbook and notes may not save you, if you can't code well.

Professor Michel clearly expects a lot out of his students, as evidenced by his exams which are extremely hard. Yet at the same time, his generous curves demonstrate that he understands how insanely hard the exams are. His lectures are very engaging and he does very well at explaining concepts that are both difficult to learn and to teach.

I got an A out of this class, but had to work pretty hard for it. Significant curve as my actual average was a 78. Be ready for a lot of long nights doing projects. Start early. If you do well on the homeworks and merely pass the exams, you'll get an A.

Professor: Laurent Michel
Very tough class. Got a 20% on the midterm (and I have an A- homework/project average). Still definitely learned a lot and he's willing to help you if you ask, he just expects a lot from his students.

I almost failed the class because of how difficult it was, however he is one of the best lecturers at the University. He runs an extremely tight ship in terms of assignments and exams. The first exam average was around a 20%, and half the class got a 0 on the second exam, including myself. At least 50% dropped it.

Laurent definitely knows his stuff but his expectations are unbelievably high. Exams were not on par with homework. For the most part, partial credit wasn't given on homework. If the program worked incorrectly, sometimes credit was given. If the program didn't compile, no credit was given (this was also true for exams which were in person coding).

Professor: Laurent Michel
Good teaching, can go very fast at times but not an issue if you ask or get help outside of class. Cares for your success. He says you will do good if you practice and that's definitely true. Attending class helps but only if you actually try to follow the slides. The slides are fine.

Laurent's courses are very difficult, but he makes up for that by being a really good professor. He cares a lot about your success and is always willing to help if you're putting in the effort. I recommend taking a course with him if you have the chance.

Class difficulty grows exponentially with time. Do well on early hw assignments, so your hw average stays high. He is also willing to extend assignments if u ask. He drops two lowest hw assignments, which is great. The exams are tough. You need to study and practice a lot if you want to do good on them. Gives some small exam hints in lectures.

Professor: Laurent Michel
Very good professor. Teaches course material in a very clear and appropriate manner. Expects you to EARN your grade. Exams are hard (as others have mentioned) but if you put in the hours necessary for practicing the material and doing the assignments *independently* then an A is definitely attainable

Professor: Lina Kloub
Overall rating: 4.7/5 | Would take again (%): 94% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 2.8 | Top tags: Amazing lectures, Group projects, Mandatory attendance

Professor: Lina Kloub
she's so sweet! her lectures were super helpful and gave us a lot of grading opportunities (labs, participation, group project, and exams). definitely would recommend to anyone taking the course.

Lina Kloub is an amazing professor, and honestly, one of the best instructors I have come across yet.She teaches concepts visually and makes each lecture engaging. The course does feel a bit heavy with several different assignments, but I wish I could have her as a professor for every single CS course I have to take in the future-she is that great!

While the exams might seem a little difficult, they were fair, and all material she goes over in the slides and lecture. No homework, and we are given ample amount of time to finish our labs.

She is highly organized, posting all lecture notes, code, and even videos on Blackboard. She is also approachable and patient, and most importantly, she has a strong ability to break down complex ideas into manageable pieces that are easy to understand and retain. Great teacher, overall no complaints.

Professor: Lina Kloub
Professor Kloub taught a very hard class but made it very easy and simple. Going to lectures helped make it way easier. Also, all the notes were on huskyct so it was easy to review and practice.

Professor Kloub is great! Active and engaged inside and outside the classroom. Genuinely wants to see her students succeed, and balances out exams with lots of project grades. I'm usually not a great test taker, but her study materials were great and exams were manageable. Homeworks were a reasonable length.

I loved Professor Kloub's class! Her slides and lectures were very informative, and the tests were fine if you did the in-class worksheets and homeworks and understood them. Overall, I would definitely recommend!

Professor: Lina Kloub
This class was so hard. no opportunities for extra credit, TAs grade horrible, harshly, no partial credit ever given, exams are difficult— not enough prep for them. They don't care about you passing or not, do not recommend her at all. No curves either. Never seen cs professor not curve knowing it's hardest major + one of hardest classes at UConn.

Kloub's teaching of this course was probably the best course I've taken at UConn so far. The class made sense with her lectures, and the TA review for the midterms was also really helpful. I enjoyed the Project-Based Learning aspect of the class, giving us experience building a full-stack project. The labs are actually helpful and applicable.

Professor: Lina Kloub
I would absolutely have Lina as my professor for all CS courses! She's one of the best professors I have had and it feels embarrassing that I didn't have her for CSE2050 or CSE3500. Her CSE2102 is an easy class and she's pretty flexible. The labs, the homeworks, and the group project weren't too difficult but did require effort. I recommend her.

Professor: Olga Glebova
Overall rating: 2.2/5 | Would take again (%): 30% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.2 | Top tags: Lots of homework, Get ready to read, Lectures not helpful

Professor: Olga Glebova
Lectures are not helpful at all, grade is extreamly exam heavy with average being in the 60s. If you do not have previous CS experience you will struggle.

She is not a very understanding professor, was unkind and frankly rude to me when discussing basic accommodations involving the CSD when I have had zero issues with any other professor. There is homework due literally every 2 days for no good reason just not a good experience overall.

The class itself was ok, and for the most part was easy. As long as you do the zybooks, you should do ok on the exams. However, the zybooks do take forever to do, so don't wait till the last minute to do them. Her lectures were pretty pointless as she would just babble about random stuff the whole time. Labs were an easy 100.

Professor: Olga Glebova
This class is relatively difficult and homework heavy. The hw is online and is quite tedious but helps substantially in understanding the hard material. Attendance is not mandatory neither are the labs but they should be completed as they account for a good part of the grade. Overall, if you do the zybooks hw and try to understand it, its not bad.

Zybooks homework are absolutely ridiculous. Exams get progressively more difficult as you go through the course and very minimal extra credit. If you haven't coded before it is very difficult to keep up. Olga is a very nice and understanding professor, I just think its too difficult for an introductory course.

She's hard to follow. There's homework but its all on zybooks and it isn't helpful at all when it comes to the tests. 2500 is already hard and she didn't do much to make it easier. Even if you go to her privately and ask questions, she isn't helpful. She can be a bit rude too.

Professor: Olga Glebova
She is unbelievably nice and passionate about her classes. Seriously, go talk to her about anything in the class. Additionally she helped cut me a deal that saved my grade. If you put in effort and match her energy, she will be one of the best professors you will ever have. Still, I think her lectures are a bit tough to follow

Goated professor. Her lectures and class activities are engaging, and she really cares about helping students understand the material. You'll learn a lot just by participating, and the examples in class are often directly related to the exams. Super nice, too.

The exams were not graded fairly in my opinion , the TAs were harsh graders too. I did not really like the way lectures were taught.

5 exams which was really annoying got to a point where we had one every 3 lectures. 1 got dropped. I'd say she was a good teacher and easy homework was a large part of the grade.

Professor: Swamy Narayan Jignaas Pattipati
Overall rating: 4.3/5 | Would take again (%): 86% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.6 | Top tags: Caring, Tough grader, Amazing lectures

Professor: Swamy Narayan Jignaas Pattipati
He was a very good professor who despite being new motivated his students.

He's pretty understanding of circumstances that may prevent work from getting done, and allows you to make it up if you have a valid reason for it. He's accessible outside of class, and even in the lectures he often asks for input on his teaching style, to see if it actually helps us learn.

Professor Jignaas Pattipati brings great energy to class, and really encourages his students to participate in lecture by asking/answering questions, which creates an environment conducive to learning. My only "complaint" (which I found not to be that bad) was the fact that he clicks through the lecture slides really fast, but to each their own.

Professor: Swamy Narayan Jignaas Pattipati
Best prof for CSE3100. If you are willing to learn, you will gain a lot. He is passionate and truly cares about student success. He prepares you for more than just exams. He is very strict about ai,do not use it, you will get caught. Sometimes explanations can be unclear, but he is very accessible outside of class and helpful in office hours

He clearly cares about his teaching but he doesn't actually do anything in lecture to help students. Lot's of extra credit opportunities and he is very strict about AI usage. He has potential but he cannot lecture to save his life so I had to learn everything myself.

The course along with the exams and hw are pretty tough however Swamy knows this and takes it into account while grading. Show up to lecture, pay attention and you'll be rewarded. Dude is hilarious too, always looked forward to lecture cause it was like going to a comedy club. Don't use AI, code on your own, ask for help.

Professor: Swamy Narayan Jignaas Pattipati
Swamy is a chill guy, his lectures are really good. Takes the time to answer questions. Exams are hard asf but he is forgiving with his grading for the most part, just don't cheat or get caught or he WILL get you. If you go to lecture and take time to do the homework you should be fine.

People that are giving awful ratings either got caught cheating, or only use AI on HW (btw Wei Wei makes the HW). I took this class in Fall 24 and although he can get lectures done pretty quick, he went the extra mile with sample code and was really understanding when I got sick before the first exam with catching me up and rescheduling.

Professor: Swamy Narayan Jignaas Pattipati
Overall not a bad professor but it's a hard class that no matter what you need to put effort if you want to get a decent grade. Exams are extremely difficult and formatted to catch cheating. Super strict on Ai/cheating. Very overhyped in my opinion and can be rude but as long as you take the time to learn the material you should be fine.

Swamy is a goated lecturer. His lectures are interesting and engaging, w/slides posted on HuskyCT. However, his grading can be tough. For exams, write simple answers that go into depth but not too much as extra details can be marked wrong. At the time of this review, an exam replacement was offered for the final; don't bank on it, though. GL!

Professor: Timothy Curry
Overall rating: 4.1/5 | Would take again (%): 77% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 3.4 | Top tags: Amazing lectures, Test heavy, Helpful

Professor: Timothy Curry
Great guy but not great professor. It's basically a teach yourself kind of class. He gives homework due one week and the next tries to teach us about it. It's too little too late. Tests make no sense and labs are pointless. I am also going to be so real when I say the guy talks in exclamation points. Goodluck and Godspeed.

Prof. Curry was a great professor but the way the class was structured makes his role limited. At least in the Fall 2024 semester, there was only 50-minute lecture a week, so you didn't have much time to interact with him. He was understanding and you could tell he was passionate about what he was teaching, so do reach out to him if you need help!

Professor: Timothy Curry
Course material is tough if you have never coded before but professor Curry makes concepts very easy to understand during his lectures. Unfortunately most of the content is learned through videos rather than his in-person lectures and is more like an online course. Do the homework and you'll be fine (they're also weighted the same as exams)

The material he teaches is difficult and that doesn't change, but he makes a great effort to teach and dissect everything to a newbie level. He is very caring and will always offer help when asked. If you could take any class with him, pick him if you want to get a decent grade/understanding of the material. But you also need to put the effort too!

It's very refreshing to have an instructor that is as passionate about what he does and is actually invested in students' learning. 2050 is a difficult class, but if you participate it makes the learning much easier, especially with an instructor like Curry. Highly recommend Curry!

Professor: Timothy Curry
Tim Curry is a great guy with a great personality and very passionate, but unfortunately passion doesn't translate to teaching ability. Always has good slides, but doesn't give good lectures. The class is difficult but if you stay on top of things its manageable. Makes you do a few microsoft forms for attendance each class.

He deleted my first 3 ratings, however, he is a very boring professor who relies too much on his slides which oftentimes are completely unhelpful when it comes to doing the homework. His class and way of teaching is very boring. TA's are unhelp apart from Victor Lek, and Discord helps at the start but as the semester progresses TA's abandon it.

Curry is the goat but this class is vry hard.

Professor: Timothy Curry
Curry is the goat but this class is vry hard.

Good professor, good lectures, class overall was fairly easy, too easy. If you are deciding between 3150 or 3160 take this class, its A LOT less work. midterm was live coding, final was 17 pages of coding on paper. labs and hw were all easy. only issue i had with his teaching style was the FORCED attendance (10% of grade) via in-class polls.

Prof. Curry is good for CSE 3140! Lectures are helpful to understand the labs

Professor: Zhijie 'Jerry Shi
Overall rating: 2.5/5 | Would take again (%): 40% | Level of difficulty (1-5): 4.2 | Top tags: Test heavy, Lots of homework, Participation matters

Professor: Zhijie 'Jerry Shi
Wow, what an underrated professor. I was afraid to take this class with him as I had a conflict with Scoggin's, but I had nothing to worry about. Shi does a great job presenting the content, answering questions, and stimulating interest in computer architecture. Give him a chance, REALLY participate, and you will see him as I do!

What a waste of time and money. Lectures are bad, along with slides, HW and labs. Tough exams weighted 70% with 50 to 60% averages. No curve. No effort is put into improving the course.

NEVER TAKE 3666 WITH THIS GUY. exams are weighting 70% of your overall grade, so you could pass this course without attending the lad and lecture and you could forget about the lab and homework if you can do good on the exams, and he is always mumbling during the lecture, he just enjoy watching students suffer. worst CS professor ever.

Professor: Zhijie 'Jerry Shi
His exams are straightforward and cover exactly what you'd expect based on the material. The lectures are pretty clear. He is not that bad.

The one comment on here that is 5 stars is from one of the TA's This is a hard class. Jerry Shi was involved in a shady scandal 10 years ago. He is hard to understand during lecture and it's required. He hates answering questions. We were promised a final grade curve, but after the final, he just ghosted anyone who asked about it.

This class is bad, but Jerry makes it worse. I would show up to the other professor's lectures because I literally could not understand Jerry's rambling. He's not an evil person and his exams aren't tougher, but you won't get anything out of his lectures because he doesn't explain anything well, his extra credit was harder, and his curve was less.

Professor: Zhijie 'Jerry Shi
Boy oh boy, where do I even begin? Professor Jerry Shi, my guiding light. I have admired you ever since I first attended your Computer Science Architecture class. The way you break down complex systems, it's like watching a master artist at work. If only I could freeze time, so I'd never have to witness the day you decide to retire from teaching.

Jerry is actually a solid CSE professor. He teaches too fast sometimes, but my god is he accessible thru piazza like 24/7. Exams are, imo, easier than cse2050 and homeworks aren't too difficult. He uploads demo code to his Github which is nice to reference. I didn't have high expectations for Jerry but was pleasantly surprised and would reccommend!

The number of things wrong with this class are kind of incredible. Absolutely terrible lectures, as the professor struggles to form even a single coherent thought, leaving every student learning absolutely nothing. Slides are atrocious, and the only textbook referenced is the worst resource they could have chosen. Genuinely disappointing. Avoid.

Professor: Zhijie 'Jerry Shi
Jerry is very friendly and has a good sense of humor. You can tell he actually cares about whether you learn the subject matter. He will test you in class to make sure you comprehend, and drill it into your head if you don't. The homeworks were actually helpful and let you practice the concepts you learned in class.
